      const disk = pf.disk_free_gb != null ? `${pf.disk_free_gb} GB free` : "";
      const cacheTxt = pf.model_cache
        ? (typeof pf.model_cache === "string" ? pf.model_cache : "cached")
        : "model not yet cached (will download on first use)";
      el.pfRows.appendChild(pfRow("Model cache & disk space", cls, [cacheTxt, disk].filter(Boolean).join(" Â· ")));
    }
    // HF terms
    el.pfRows.appendChild(pfRow("Hugging Face model terms", pf.hf_terms_ok ? "ok" : "warn",
      pf.hf_terms_ok ? "accepted" : "not accepted â€” some models may be unavailable"));
    // CLI availability
    el.pfRows.appendChild(pfRow("Command-line tools", pfBadge(pf.cli_available),
      pf.cli_available === false ? "not on PATH" : ""));

    // Preset explanation
    el.pfPreset.className = "pf-preset";
    el.pfPreset.textContent = "";
    const preset = pf.preset || (state.settings && state.settings.hardware_preset) || "unknown";
    const name = document.createElement("div");
    name.innerHTML = `Auto-selected hardware preset: <span class="p-name"></span>`;
    name.querySelector(".p-name").textContent = preset;
    el.pfPreset.appendChild(name);
    const explain = document.createElement("div");
    explain.className = "muted";
    explain.style.marginTop = "4px";
    explain.textContent = presetExplain(preset);
    el.pfPreset.appendChild(explain);
    if (String(preset).toLowerCase() === "cpu") {
      el.pfPreset.classList.add("cpu-warn");
      const w = document.createElement("div");
      w.className = "warn-text";
      w.textContent = "No GPU detected. Real-time transcription, live translation, and speaker labels are limited on CPU; transcripts may lag or run after the session.";
      el.pfPreset.appendChild(w);
    }
  }
  function presetExplain(p) {
    switch (String(p).toLowerCase()) {
      case "cpu": return "Chosen because no supported GPU was found.";
      case "cuda": case "gpu": return "A CUDA GPU was found and will be used for real-time transcription.";
      default: return "Selected automatically based on the detected hardware.";
    }
  }
  el.pfRefresh.addEventListener("click", runPreflight);
  el.pfContinue.addEventListener("click", () => { el.preflight.hidden = true; setView("compact"); });

  /* ============================ SETTINGS DRAWER ============================ */
  function openSettings() {
    el.settings.hidden = false;
    buildSettings();
  }
  el.xSettings.addEventListener("click", openSettings);
  el.setClose.addEventListener("click", () => { el.settings.hidden = true; });
  el.settings.addEventListener("click", (e) => { if (e.target === el.settings) el.settings.hidden = true; });

  // Small builders for settings controls.
  function rowSelect(label, sub, value, options, onChange) {
    const row = mkRow(label, sub);
    const sel = document.createElement("select");
    for (const opt of options) {
      const o = document.createElement("option");
      o.value = typeof opt === "string" ? opt : opt.value;
      o.textContent = typeof opt === "string" ? opt : opt.label;
      if (o.value === String(value)) o.selected = true;
      sel.appendChild(o);
    }
    sel.addEventListener("change", () => onChange(sel.value));
    row.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(sel);
    return row;
  }
  function rowToggle(label, sub, checked, onChange) {
    const row = mkRow(label, sub);
    const wrap = document.createElement("label");
    wrap.className = "switch";
    const input = document.createElement("input");
    input.type = "checkbox";
    input.checked = !!checked;
    const track = document.createElement("span");
    track.className = "track";
    wrap.append(input, track);
    input.addEventListener("change", () => onChange(input.checked));
    row.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(wrap);
    return row;
  }
  function rowText(label, sub, value, onCommit, opts = {}) {
    const row = mkRow(label, sub);
    const input = document.createElement("input");
    input.type = opts.number ? "number" : "text";
    input.value = value != null ? value : "";
    if (opts.min != null) input.min = opts.min;
    input.addEventListener("change", () => onCommit(opts.number ? Number(input.value) : input.value));
    row.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(input);
    return row;
  }
  function rowReadonly(label, sub, value) {
    const row = mkRow(label, sub);
    row.classList.add("readonly");
    const v = document.createElement("span");
    v.className = "val";
    v.textContent = value != null && value !== "" ? String(value) : "â€”";
    row.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(v);
    return row;
  }
  function mkRow(label, sub) {
    const row = document.createElement("div");
    row.className = "set-row";
    const l = document.createElement("div");
    l.className = "lbl";
    l.textContent = label;
    if (sub) { const s = document.createElement("small"); s.textContent = sub; l.appendChild(s); }
    const c = document.createElement("div");
    c.className = "ctl";
    row.append(l, c);
    return row;
  }
  function group(title) {
    const g = document.createElement("div");
    g.className = "set-group";
    const h = document.createElement("h3");
    h.textContent = title;
    g.appendChild(h);
    return g;
  }

  async function putSetting(patch) {
    try {
      const updated = await api("/api/settings", { method: "PUT", body: patch });
      state.settings = updated || Object.assign(state.settings || {}, patch);
      applyTheme();
    } catch (e) {
      notice("Couldn't save setting: " + (e.message || e), "error");
    }
  }

  function buildSettings() {
    const s = state.settings || {};
    el.setBody.textContent = "";

    /* --- Hardware --- */
    const gHw = group("Hardware");
    gHw.appendChild(rowReadonly("Detected preset", "auto-selected at launch", s.hardware_preset));
    gHw.appendChild(rowSelect("Whisper model", null, s.whisper_model,
      ["tiny", "base", "small", "medium", "large-v3", s.whisper_model].filter(uniq),
      (v) => putSetting({ whisper_model: v })));
    gHw.appendChild(rowSelect("Compute type", null, s.whisper_compute_type,
      ["int8", "int8_float16", "float16", "float32", s.whisper_compute_type].filter(uniq),
      (v) => putSetting({ whisper_compute_type: v })));
    gHw.appendChild(rowSelect("Latency mode", null, s.latency_mode,
      ["low", "balanced", "accurate", s.latency_mode].filter(uniq),
      (v) => putSetting({ latency_mode: v })));
    el.setBody.appendChild(gHw);

    /* --- Translation --- */
    const gTr = group("Translation");
    gTr.appendChild(rowToggle("Translate", "show a translation line under each utterance",
      s.translate_enabled, (v) => putSetting({ translate_enabled: v })));
    gTr.appendChild(rowSelect("Provider", null, s.translation_provider,
      ["local", "gemini", s.translation_provider].filter(uniq),
      (v) => putSetting({ translation_provider: v })));
    gTr.appendChild(rowSelect("Device", null, s.translation_device,
      ["auto", "cuda", "cpu", s.translation_device].filter(uniq),
      (v) => putSetting({ translation_device: v })));
    el.setBody.appendChild(gTr);

    /* --- Speakers --- */
    const gSp = group("Speaker labels");
    gSp.appendChild(rowToggle("Diarization", "identify who is speaking",
      s.diarization_enabled, (v) => putSetting({ diarization_enabled: v })));
    gSp.appendChild(rowToggle("Real-time labels", "label speakers live (needs GPU)",
      s.diarization_realtime, (v) => putSetting({ diarization_realtime: v })));
    el.setBody.appendChild(gSp);

    /* --- Storage --- */
    const gStore = group("Storage");
    gStore.appendChild(rowText("Sessions folder", null, s.sessions_root,
      (v) => putSetting({ sessions_root: v })));
    gStore.appendChild(rowText("Retention (days)", "0 keeps sessions forever", s.retention_days,
      (v) => putSetting({ retention_days: v }), { number: true, min: 0 }));
    el.setBody.appendChild(gStore);

    /* --- Appearance --- */
    const gAppr = group("Appearance");
    gAppr.appendChild(rowSelect("Theme", null, s.theme || "auto",
      [{ value: "auto", label: "Auto (system)" }, { value: "light", label: "Light" }, { value: "dark", label: "Dark" }],
      (v) => putSetting({ theme: v })));
    el.setBody.appendChild(gAppr);

    /* --- Secrets --- */
    const gSec = group("Secrets");
    gSec.appendChild(secretRow("Hugging Face token", "hf_token", s.hf_token_is_set));
    gSec.appendChild(secretRow("Gemini API key", "gemini_api_key", s.gemini_api_key_is_set));
    el.setBody.appendChild(gSec);

    /* --- Sessions --- */
    const gSess = group("Sessions");
    const list = document.createElement("div");
    list.className = "sess-list";
    list.id = "sess-list";
    list.textContent = "Loadingâ€¦";
    gSess.appendChild(list);
    el.setBody.appendChild(gSess);
    loadSessionsInto();

    /* --- Later versions (disabled) --- */
    const gM4 = group("Post-processing");
    const summarize = mkRow("Summarize session", "available in a later version");
    const sBtn = document.createElement("button");
    sBtn.className = "btn"; sBtn.textContent = "Summarize"; sBtn.disabled = true;
    summarize.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(sBtn);
    gM4.appendChild(summarize);
    const rediar = mkRow("Re-diarize", "available in a later version");
    const rBtn = document.createElement("button");
    rBtn.className = "btn"; rBtn.textContent = "Re-diarize"; rBtn.disabled = true;
    rediar.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(rBtn);
    gM4.appendChild(rediar);
    el.setBody.appendChild(gM4);

    /* --- Legal --- */
    const gLegal = group("Legal");
    const legal = mkRow("Consent notice", "review the recording terms");
    const lBtn = document.createElement("button");
    lBtn.className = "link-btn"; lBtn.textContent = "Legal & Consent";
    lBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { el.consent.hidden = false; });
    legal.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(lBtn);
    gLegal.appendChild(legal);
    if (s.app_version) gLegal.appendChild(rowReadonly("App version", null, s.app_version));
    el.setBody.appendChild(gLegal);
  }

  function uniq(v, i, arr) { return v != null && v !== "" && arr.indexOf(v) === i; }

  function secretRow(label, name, isSet) {
    const row = mkRow(label, isSet ? null : "not configured");
    const state_span = document.createElement("span");
    state_span.className = "secret-state " + (isSet ? "set" : "unset");
    state_span.textContent = isSet ? "is set" : "not set";
    const setBtn = document.createElement("button");
    setBtn.className = "link-btn";
    setBtn.textContent = isSet ? "Replace" : "Set";
    setBtn.addEventListener("click", () => setSecret(name, label));
    const ctl = row.querySelector(".ctl");
    ctl.appendChild(state_span);
    ctl.appendChild(setBtn);
    if (isSet) {
      const clrBtn = document.createElement("button");
      clrBtn.className = "link-btn danger-link";
      clrBtn.style.color = "var(--fail)";
      clrBtn.textContent = "Clear";
      clrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => clearSecret(name, label));
      ctl.appendChild(clrBtn);
    }
    return row;
  }

  async function setSecret(name, label) {
    const value = window.prompt(`Enter ${label}. It is stored write-only and never shown again.`);
    if (value == null) return;
    const v = value.trim();
    if (!v) return;
    try {
      await api(`/api/secrets/${name}`, { method: "POST", body: { value: v } });
      notice(`${label} saved.`, "info");
      await refreshSettings();
      buildSettings();
    } catch (e) { notice(`Couldn't save ${label}: ` + (e.message || e), "error"); }
  }
  async function clearSecret(name, label) {
    confirmDialog(`Clear the stored ${label}? This cannot be undone.`, "Clear", async () => {
      try {
        await api(`/api/secrets/${name}`, { method: "DELETE" });
        notice(`${label} cleared.`, "info");
        await refreshSettings();
        buildSettings();
      } catch (e) { notice(`Couldn't clear ${label}: ` + (e.message || e), "error"); }
    });
  }

  /* ============================ SESSIONS LIST ============================ */
  async function loadSessionsInto() {
    const list = document.getElementById("sess-list");
    if (!list) return; // drawer not open
    let sessions;
    try { sessions = await api("/api/sessions"); }
    catch (e) { list.textContent = ""; const p = document.createElement("div"); p.className = "sess-empty"; p.textContent = "Couldn't load sessions."; list.appendChild(p); return; }

    list.textContent = "";
    if (!sessions || !sessions.length) {
      const p = document.createElement("div");
      p.className = "sess-empty";
      p.textContent = "No sessions yet.";
      list.appendChild(p);
      return;
    }
    for (const meta of sessions) {
      list.appendChild(sessionItem(meta));
    }
  }

  function sessionItem(meta) {
    const item = document.createElement("div");
    item.className = "sess-item";
    const top = document.createElement("div");
    top.className = "s-top";
    const title = document.createElement("div");
    title.className = "s-title";
    title.textContent = meta.title || "Untitled session";
    const m = document.createElement("div");
    m.className = "s-meta";
    m.textContent = [fmtDate(meta.created_at), fmtDur(meta.duration_sec)].filter(Boolean).join(" Â· ");
    top.append(title, m);

    const actions = document.createElement("div");
    actions.className = "s-actions";
    const delAudio = document.createElement("button");
    delAudio.className = "link-btn";
    delAudio.textContent = "Delete audio only";
    delAudio.addEventListener("click", () => {
      confirmDialog(`Delete the audio file for "${meta.title || "this session"}"? The transcript is kept.`, "Delete audio", async () => {
        try { await api(`/api/sessions/${meta.session_id}/audio`, { method: "DELETE" }); notice("Audio deleted.", "info"); }
        catch (e) { notice("Couldn't delete audio: " + (e.message || e), "error"); }
      });
    });
    const delAll = document.createElement("button");
    delAll.className = "link-btn danger-link";
    delAll.style.color = "var(--fail)";
    delAll.textContent = "Delete session";
    delAll.addEventListener("click", () => {
      confirmDialog(`Permanently delete "${meta.title || "this session"}" and its transcript? This cannot be undone.`, "Delete", async () => {
        try { await api(`/api/sessions/${meta.session_id}`, { method: "DELETE" }); notice("Session deleted.", "info"); loadSessionsInto(); }
        catch (e) { notice("Couldn't delete session: " + (e.message || e), "error"); }
      });
    });
    actions.append(delAudio, delAll);
    item.append(top, actions);
    return item;
  }

  /* ============================ CONFIRM DIALOG ============================ */
  let confirmHandler = null;
  function confirmDialog(message, okLabel, onOk) {
    el.confirmMsg.textContent = message;
    el.confirmOk.textContent = okLabel || "Confirm";
    confirmHandler = onOk;
    el.confirm.hidden = false;
  }
  el.confirmCancel.addEventListener("click", () => { el.confirm.hidden = true; confirmHandler = null; });
  el.confirmOk.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    const h = confirmHandler;
    el.confirm.hidden = true; confirmHandler = null;
    if (h) await h();
  });

  /* ============================ THEME ============================ */
  function applyTheme() {
    const theme = (state.settings && state.settings.theme) || "auto";
    document.documentElement.setAttribute("data-theme", theme);
  }

  /* ============================ WEBSOCKET ============================ */
  function wsUrl() {
    const proto = window.location.protocol === "https:" ? "wss:" : "ws:";
    return `${proto}//${window.location.host}/ws?token=${encodeURIComponent(TOKEN || "")}`;
  }

  function connectWs() {
    let ws;
    try { ws = new WebSocket(wsUrl()); }
    catch (e) { scheduleReconnect(); return; }
    state.ws = ws;

    ws.addEventListener("open", () => {
      state.wsBackoff = 500;
      // On reconnect, replay durable events we missed by seq.
      if (state.everConnected && state.lastSeq > 0) catchUp(state.lastSeq);
      state.everConnected = true;
      // Ask the server for a fresh status snapshot.
      requestStatus();
    });

    ws.addEventListener("message", (ev) => {
      let msg;
      try { msg = JSON.parse(ev.data); } catch (_) { return; }
      handleWsMessage(msg);
    });

    ws.addEventListener("close", () => { state.ws = null; scheduleReconnect(); });
    ws.addEventListener("error", () => { try { ws.close(); } catch (_) {} });
  }

  function scheduleReconnect() {
    clearTimeout(state.wsTimer);
    const delay = state.wsBackoff;
    state.wsBackoff = Math.min(state.wsBackoff * 2, 10000);
    state.wsTimer = setTimeout(connectWs, delay);
  }

  function requestStatus() {
    // Prefer a WS request; fall back to REST if the socket isn't open.
    if (state.ws && state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      try { state.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "get_status" })); return; } catch (_) {}
    }
    api("/api/capture/status").then(applyStatusSnapshot).catch(() => {});
  }

  function applyStatusSnapshot(st) {
    if (!st) return;
    if (typeof st.recording === "boolean") setRecording(st.recording);
    if (st.session_id) state.sessionId = st.session_id;
    renderStatus(st);
  }

  function handleWsMessage(msg) {
    switch (msg.type) {
      case "utterance":
        if (msg.record) addUtterance(msg.record);
        break;
      case "patch":
        if (msg.seq != null) {
          if (msg.seq > state.lastSeq) state.lastSeq = msg.seq;
          patchUtterance(msg.seq, msg.fields || {});
        }
        break;
      case "status":
        if (typeof msg.recording === "boolean") setRecording(msg.recording);
        renderStatus(msg);
        break;
      case "rename":
        renameSpeaker(msg.old, msg.new);
        break;
      case "error":
        notice(`${msg.message || "Error"}${msg.code ? " (" + msg.code + ")" : ""}`, "error");
        break;
      default:
        // Unknown message types are ignored on purpose.
        break;
    }
  }

  // Replay durable utterance events by seq after a reconnect gap.
  async function catchUp(sinceSeq) {
    if (!state.sessionId) return;
    try {
      const rows = await api(`/api/sessions/${state.sessionId}/utterances?since_seq=${sinceSeq}`);
      if (Array.isArray(rows)) for (const rec of rows) addUtterance(rec);
    } catch (e) { /* best-effort catch-up */ }
  }

  /* ============================ BOOT ============================ */
  async function refreshSettings() {
    state.settings = await api("/api/settings");
    state.consentOk = !!(state.settings && state.settings.consent_acknowledged);
    applyTheme();
    refreshToggleEnabled();
    return state.settings;
  }

  async function boot() {
    if (!TOKEN) { el.noToken.hidden = false; return; }
    el.app.hidden = false;

    // 1) Settings drive consent + theme.
    try {
      await refreshSettings();
    } catch (e) {
      notice("Couldn't reach the local service. Some features may be unavailable.", "error");
      state.settings = {};
    }

    // 2) Consent gate.
    if (!state.consentOk) openConsent();

    // 3) Preflight before the first record.
    el.preflight.hidden = false;
    runPreflight();

    // 4) Current capture status (in case a session is already running).
    try { applyStatusSnapshot(await api("/api/capture/status")); } catch (_) {}
    renderRecent();

    // 5) Live stream.
    connectWs();

    // Default to compact bar underneath the overlays.
    setView("compact");
  }

  document.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
    if (e.key === "Escape") {
      if (!el.settings.hidden) el.settings.hidden = true;
      else if (!el.confirm.hidden) { el.confirm.hidden = true; confirmHandler = null; }
    }
  });

  boot();
})();

codex
The current UI already has compact/expanded views, tokenized REST/WS helpers, consent/preflight, settings, transcript rows, and patch handling. I’m going to inspect the rest of `app.js` and the web-only diff before editing so I don’t disturb unrelated work.

   1: /* ai-record â€” front-end controller (vanilla JS, no deps).
   2:  *
   3:  * Responsibilities:
   4:  *   - Read the per-launch token from the URL and attach it to every REST + WS call.
   5:  *   - Drive four surfaces: consent modal, preflight, compact bar, expanded view.
   6:  *   - Maintain a live transcript from the WebSocket (STT-first, patched in place).
   7:  *   - Bind a settings drawer to GET/PUT /api/settings and the secrets endpoints.
   8:  *
   9:  * The whole UI is one window; "expand"/"collapse" swaps views + asks pywebview
  10:  * (if present) to resize. It never opens a second window.
  11:  */
  12: (() => {
  13:   "use strict";
  14: 
  15:   /* ============================ AUTH / TOKEN ============================ */
  16:   const TOKEN = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get("token");
  17:   const HEADER = "X-AI-Record-Token";
  18: 
  19:   /* ============================ APP STATE ============================ */
  20:   const state = {
  21:     settings: null,          // last redacted settings object
  22:     consentOk: false,
  23:     recording: false,
  24:     sessionId: null,
  25:     lastSeq: 0,              // highest durable seq seen (for WS catch-up)
  26:     utterances: new Map(),   // seq -> {record, el}
  27:     autoScroll: true,
  28:     searchTerm: "",
  29:     ws: null,
  30:     wsBackoff: 500,
  31:     wsTimer: null,
  32:     everConnected: false,
  33:   };
  34: 
  35:   const MAX_ROWS = 500;      // cap DOM nodes for very long transcripts
  36: 
  37:   /* ============================ DOM SHORTCUTS ============================ */
  38:   const $ = (id) => document.getElementById(id);
  39:   const el = {
  40:     app: $("app"),
  41:     // compact
  42:     cToggle: $("c-toggle"), cDot: $("c-dot"), cStatusText: $("c-status-text"),
  43:     cStatus: $("c-status"), cRecent: $("c-recent"), cExpand: $("c-expand"),
  44:     // expanded
  45:     xCollapse: $("x-collapse"), xTitle: $("x-title"), xToggle: $("x-toggle"),
  46:     xDot: $("x-dot"), xStatusText: $("x-status-text"), xStatus: $("x-status"),
  47:     xChips: $("x-chips"), xSearch: $("x-search"), xSettings: $("x-settings"),
  48:     xTranscript: $("x-transcript"), xJump: $("x-jump"),
  49:     // overlays
  50:     consent: $("consent"), consentAgree: $("consent-agree"),
  51:     preflight: $("preflight"), pfRows: $("pf-rows"), pfPreset: $("pf-preset"),
  52:     pfContinue: $("pf-continue"), pfRefresh: $("pf-refresh"),
  53:     settings: $("settings"), setClose: $("set-close"), setBody: $("set-body"),
  54:     confirm: $("confirm"), confirmMsg: $("confirm-msg"),
  55:     confirmOk: $("confirm-ok"), confirmCancel: $("confirm-cancel"),
  56:     noToken: $("no-token"),
  57:     notices: $("notices"),
  58:   };
  59: 
  60:   /* ============================ REST HELPERS ============================ */
  61:   async function api(path, { method = "GET", body } = {}) {
  62:     const opts = { method, headers: { [HEADER]: TOKEN || "" } };
  63:     if (body !== undefined) {
  64:       opts.headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json";
  65:       opts.body = JSON.stringify(body);
  66:     }
  67:     const res = await fetch(path, opts);
  68:     if (!res.ok) {
  69:       const err = new Error(`${method} ${path} -> ${res.status}`);
  70:       err.status = res.status;
  71:       try { err.data = await res.json(); } catch (_) { /* ignore */ }
  72:       throw err;
  73:     }
  74:     if (res.status === 204) return null;
  75:     const ct = res.headers.get("content-type") || "";
  76:     return ct.includes("application/json") ? res.json() : res.text();
  77:   }
  78: 
  79:   /* ============================ NOTICES ============================ */
  80:   function notice(message, kind = "info") {
  81:     const node = document.createElement("div");
  82:     node.className = "notice" + (kind === "error" ? " error" : kind === "warn" ? " warn" : "");
  83:     const msg = document.createElement("div");
  84:     msg.className = "n-msg";
  85:     msg.textContent = message;
  86:     const close = document.createElement("button");
  87:     close.className = "n-close";
  88:     close.textContent = "âœ•";
  89:     close.setAttribute("aria-label", "Dismiss");
  90:     close.onclick = () => node.remove();
  91:     node.append(msg, close);
  92:     el.notices.appendChild(node);
  93:     if (kind !== "error") setTimeout(() => node.remove(), 6000);
  94:   }
  95: 
  96:   /* ============================ VIEW SWITCHING ============================ */
  97:   // pywebview exposes an optional resize hook; ignore if not present.
  98:   function requestResize(w, h) {
  99:     try {
 100:       const pw = window.pywebview;
 101:       if (pw && pw.api && typeof pw.api.resize === "function") pw.api.resize(w, h);
 102:     } catch (_) { /* not hosted by pywebview, or no api */ }
 103:   }
 104: 
 105:   function setView(view) {
 106:     el.app.dataset.view = view;
 107:     const expanded = view === "expanded";
 108:     $("expanded").hidden = !expanded;
 109:     $("compact").hidden = expanded;
 110:     if (expanded) { requestResize(900, 640); scrollToLatest(); }
 111:     else { requestResize(520, 120); }
 112:   }
 113: 
 114:   /* ============================ TIME / TEXT UTILS ============================ */
 115:   function fmtTs(sec) {
 116:     if (sec == null || isNaN(sec)) return "--:--";
 117:     const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor(sec));
 118:     const h = Math.floor(s / 3600), m = Math.floor((s % 3600) / 60), ss = s % 60;
 119:     const pad = (n) => String(n).padStart(2, "0");
 120:     return h > 0 ? `${h}:${pad(m)}:${pad(ss)}` : `${pad(m)}:${pad(ss)}`;
 121:   }
 122:   function fmtDur(sec) {
 123:     if (!sec && sec !== 0) return "";
 124:     const m = Math.floor(sec / 60), s = Math.round(sec % 60);
 125:     return `${m}m ${String(s).padStart(2, "0")}s`;
 126:   }
 127:   function fmtDate(iso) {
 128:     if (!iso) return "";
 129:     const d = new Date(iso);
 130:     if (isNaN(d)) return iso;
 131:     return d.toLocaleString([], { month: "short", day: "numeric", hour: "2-digit", minute: "2-digit" });
 132:   }
 133: 
 134:   /* ============================ STATUS / DEGRADED STATE ============================ */
 135:   // Map degraded_state codes to human labels + tooltip detail.
 136:   const DEGRADED = {
 137:     stt_catching_up:     { label: "STT catching up",             detail: "Speech-to-text is behind live audio and catching up." },
 138:     audio_only:          { label: "Audio-only",                  detail: "Transcription is unavailable; audio is still being captured." },
 139:     translation_paused:  { label: "Translation paused",          detail: "Translation is temporarily paused." },
 140:     diarization_offline: { label: "Speaker labels offline-only", detail: "Speaker labels will be assigned after the session, not live." },
 141:     mic_only:            { label: "Mic only",                    detail: "Only your microphone is captured; system audio is unavailable." },
 142:     them_only:           { label: "System-audio only",           detail: "Only system audio is captured; your microphone is unavailable." },
 143:   };
 144: 
 145:   function computeStatus(status) {
 146:     // Returns { text, dotState, tooltip }
 147:     if (!state.recording) return { text: "Idle", dot: "idle", tip: "" };
 148:     const codes = (status && status.degraded_states) || [];
 149:     if (status && status.error) return { text: "Error", dot: "error", tip: status.note || "" };
 150:     // Priority: pick the first known degraded state; else plain "Recording".
 151:     for (const code of codes) {
 152:       const d = DEGRADED[code];
 153:       if (d) return { text: d.label, dot: "warn", tip: d.detail };
 154:     }
 155:     if (codes.length) return { text: "Recording (degraded)", dot: "warn", tip: codes.join(", ") };
 156:     return { text: "Recording", dot: "recording", tip: "" };
 157:   }
 158: 
 159:   function renderStatus(status) {
 160:     const s = computeStatus(status || {});
 161:     for (const [dot, text, wrap] of [
 162:       [el.cDot, el.cStatusText, el.cStatus],
 163:       [el.xDot, el.xStatusText, el.xStatus],
 164:     ]) {
 165:       dot.dataset.state = s.dot;
 166:       text.textContent = s.text;
 167:       wrap.title = s.tip || s.text;
 168:     }
 169:     // Degraded-state chips (expanded only).
 170:     el.xChips.textContent = "";
 171:     const codes = (status && status.degraded_states) || [];
 172:     for (const code of codes) {
 173:       const d = DEGRADED[code];
 174:       const chip = document.createElement("span");
 175:       chip.className = "chip warn";
 176:       chip.textContent = d ? d.label : code;
 177:       chip.title = d ? d.detail : code;
 178:       el.xChips.appendChild(chip);
 179:     }
 180:     if (status && status.effective_model) {
 181:       const chip = document.createElement("span");
 182:       chip.className = "chip info";
 183:       chip.textContent = status.effective_model;
 184:       chip.title = "Effective STT model" + (status.ladder_step != null ? ` Â· ladder step ${status.ladder_step}` : "");
 185:       el.xChips.appendChild(chip);
 186:     }
 187:   }
 188: 
 189:   function setRecording(on) {
 190:     state.recording = on;
 191:     for (const btn of [el.cToggle, el.xToggle]) {
 192:       btn.classList.toggle("recording", on);
 193:       btn.textContent = on ? "â— Stop" : "Start";
 194:     }
 195:   }
 196: 
 197:   function refreshToggleEnabled() {
 198:     const enabled = state.consentOk;
 199:     for (const btn of [el.cToggle, el.xToggle]) {
 200:       btn.disabled = !enabled;
 201:       btn.title = enabled ? "" : "Acknowledge the consent notice before recording.";
 202:     }
 203:   }
 204: 
 205:   /* ============================ TRANSCRIPT RENDERING ============================ */
 206:   function speakerText(rec) {
 207:     // Unknown / low-confidence -> "?"
 208:     if (rec.speaker && rec.speaker.trim()) {
 209:       const low = rec.diarization_confidence != null && rec.diarization_confidence < 0.5;
 210:       return { text: rec.speaker, cls: low ? "unknown" : "" };
 211:     }
 212:     // No speaker yet: diarization pending placeholder.
 213:     return { text: "?", cls: "pending" };
 214:   }
 215: 
 216:   function buildRow(rec) {
 217:     const row = document.createElement("div");
 218:     row.className = "utt";
 219:     row.dataset.seq = rec.seq;
 220:     row.dataset.source = rec.source === "you" ? "you" : "them";
 221: 
 222:     const ts = document.createElement("div");
 223:     ts.className = "ts";
 224:     ts.textContent = fmtTs(rec.start);
 225: 
 226:     const spk = document.createElement("div");
 227:     spk.className = "spk";
 228:     const sp = speakerText(rec);
 229:     spk.textContent = sp.text;
 230:     if (sp.cls) spk.classList.add(sp.cls);
 231:     if (rec.diarization_confidence != null)
 232:       spk.title = `confidence ${(rec.diarization_confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}% Â· click to rename`;
 233:     else spk.title = "click to rename";
 234:     spk.addEventListener("click", () => beginRename(spk, rec));
 235: 
 236:     const body = document.createElement("div");
 237:     body.className = "body";
 238:     const text = document.createElement("div");
 239:     text.className = "text";
 240:     text.textContent = rec.text || "";
 241:     const tr = document.createElement("div");
 242:     tr.className = "tr";
 243:     applyTranslation(tr, rec);
 244:     body.append(text, tr);
 245: 
 246:     row.append(ts, spk, body);
 247:     return row;
 248:   }
 249: 
 250:   function applyTranslation(trEl, rec) {
 251:     trEl.classList.remove("pending", "error");
 252:     if (rec.translation_error) {
 253:       trEl.textContent = `Translation failed: ${rec.translation_error}`;
 254:       trEl.classList.add("error");
 255:     } else if (rec.translation) {
 256:       trEl.textContent = rec.translation;
 257:     } else if (state.settings && state.settings.translate_enabled) {
 258:       // Translation is expected but hasn't arrived yet.
 259:       trEl.textContent = "translatingâ€¦";
 260:       trEl.classList.add("pending");
 261:     } else {
 262:       trEl.textContent = "";
 263:     }
 264:   }
 265: 
 266:   function addUtterance(rec) {
 267:     if (rec.seq == null) return;
 268:     if (state.utterances.has(rec.seq)) { patchUtterance(rec.seq, rec); return; }
 269:     if (rec.seq > state.lastSeq) state.lastSeq = rec.seq;
 270: 
 271:     const row = buildRow(rec);
 272:     state.utterances.set(rec.seq, { record: rec, el: row });
 273: 
 274:     // Insert in seq order (usually just append; handle out-of-order catch-up).
 275:     const cont = el.xTranscript;
 276:     const lastChild = cont.lastElementChild;
 277:     if (!lastChild || Number(lastChild.dataset.seq) < rec.seq) {
 278:       cont.appendChild(row);
 279:     } else {
 280:       let ref = null;
 281:       for (const child of cont.children) {
 282:         if (Number(child.dataset.seq) > rec.seq) { ref = child; break; }
 283:       }
 284:       cont.insertBefore(row, ref);
 285:     }
 286: 
 287:     trimDom();
 288:     applySearchToRow(row);
 289:     renderRecent();
 290:     if (state.autoScroll) scrollToLatest();
 291:   }
 292: 
 293:   function patchUtterance(seq, fields) {
 294:     const entry = state.utterances.get(seq);
 295:     if (!entry) return;
 296:     Object.assign(entry.record, fields);
 297:     const rec = entry.record;
 298:     const row = entry.el;
 299:     // Translation line (in place, no reflow of siblings).
 300:     const tr = row.querySelector(".tr");
 301:     if (tr) applyTranslation(tr, rec);
 302:     // Speaker label.
 303:     if ("speaker" in fields || "diarization_confidence" in fields) {
 304:       const spk = row.querySelector(".spk");
 305:       const sp = speakerText(rec);
 306:       spk.textContent = sp.text;
 307:       spk.classList.remove("pending", "unknown");
 308:       if (sp.cls) spk.classList.add(sp.cls);
 309:       if (rec.diarization_confidence != null)
 310:         spk.title = `confidence ${(rec.diarization_confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}% Â· click to rename`;
 311:     }
 312:     applySearchToRow(row);
 313:     if (isRecentSeq(seq)) renderRecent();
 314:   }
 315: 
 316:   function trimDom() {
 317:     const cont = el.xTranscript;
 318:     while (cont.children.length > MAX_ROWS) {
 319:       const first = cont.firstElementChild;
 320:       const seq = Number(first.dataset.seq);
 321:       state.utterances.delete(seq);
 322:       first.remove();
 323:     }
 324:   }
 325: 
 326:   /* Rename speaker inline; broadcasts nothing itself â€” server may echo a rename. */
 327:   function beginRename(spkEl, rec) {
 328:     if (spkEl.querySelector("input")) return;
 329:     const current = rec.speaker && rec.speaker.trim() ? rec.speaker : "";
 330:     const input = document.createElement("input");
 331:     input.className = "spk-edit";
 332:     input.value = current;
 333:     input.placeholder = "Speaker name";
 334:     spkEl.textContent = "";
 335:     spkEl.appendChild(input);
 336:     input.focus();
 337:     input.select();
 338: 
 339:     const finish = (commit) => {
 340:       const val = input.value.trim();
 341:       spkEl.textContent = "";
 342:       if (commit && val && val !== current) {
 343:         renameSpeaker(current || rec.speaker, val);
 344:       } else {
 345:         const sp = speakerText(rec);
 346:         spkEl.textContent = sp.text;
 347:         spkEl.className = "spk" + (sp.cls ? " " + sp.cls : "");
 348:       }
 349:     };
 350:     input.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
 351:       if (e.key === "Enter") { e.preventDefault(); finish(true); }
 352:       else if (e.key === "Escape") { e.preventDefault(); finish(false); }
 353:     });
 354:     input.addEventListener("blur", () => finish(true));
 355:   }
 356: 
 357:   // Relabel all rows whose speaker matches `oldName` -> `newName`.
 358:   function renameSpeaker(oldName, newName) {
 359:     if (!newName) return;
 360:     for (const { record, el: row } of state.utterances.values()) {

 succeeded in 348ms:
 361:       if ((record.speaker || "") === (oldName || "")) {
 362:         record.speaker = newName;
 363:         const spk = row.querySelector(".spk");
 364:         spk.textContent = newName;
 365:         spk.classList.remove("pending", "unknown");
 366:       }
 367:     }
 368:     renderRecent();
 369:   }
 370: 
 371:   /* ============================ COMPACT RECENT LINES ============================ */
 372:   function recentEntries() {
 373:     // Last 3 utterances by seq.
 374:     const seqs = [...state.utterances.keys()].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 375:     return seqs.slice(-3).map((s) => state.utterances.get(s).record);
 376:   }
 377:   function isRecentSeq(seq) {
 378:     const seqs = [...state.utterances.keys()].sort((a, b) => a - b).slice(-3);
 379:     return seqs.includes(seq);
 380:   }
 381:   function renderRecent() {
 382:     const recs = recentEntries();
 383:     el.cRecent.textContent = "";
 384:     if (!recs.length) {
 385:       const empty = document.createElement("div");
 386:       empty.className = "recent-empty";
 387:       empty.textContent = state.recording ? "Listeningâ€¦" : "No transcript yet.";
 388:       el.cRecent.appendChild(empty);
 389:       return;
 390:     }
 391:     for (const rec of recs) {
 392:       const line = document.createElement("div");
 393:       line.className = "r-line";
 394:       const t = document.createElement("span");
 395:       t.className = "r-text";
 396:       t.textContent = rec.text || "";
 397:       line.appendChild(t);
 398:       el.cRecent.appendChild(line);
 399:       if (rec.translation) {
 400:         const tl = document.createElement("div");
 401:         tl.className = "r-line r-tr";
 402:         tl.textContent = rec.translation;
 403:         el.cRecent.appendChild(tl);
 404:       }
 405:     }
 406:   }
 407: 
 408:   /* ============================ AUTO-SCROLL ============================ */
 409:   function scrollToLatest() {
 410:     const c = el.xTranscript;
 411:     c.scrollTop = c.scrollHeight;
 412:     state.autoScroll = true;
 413:     el.xJump.hidden = true;
 414:   }
 415:   el.xTranscript.addEventListener("scroll", () => {
 416:     const c = el.xTranscript;
 417:     const nearBottom = c.scrollHeight - c.scrollTop - c.clientHeight < 40;
 418:     state.autoScroll = nearBottom;
 419:     el.xJump.hidden = nearBottom;
 420:   });
 421:   el.xJump.addEventListener("click", scrollToLatest);
 422: 
 423:   /* ============================ SEARCH ============================ */
 424:   function highlight(node, plain, term) {
 425:     node.textContent = "";
 426:     if (!term) { node.textContent = plain; return; }
 427:     const low = plain.toLowerCase();
 428:     let i = 0, idx;
 429:     const t = term.toLowerCase();
 430:     while ((idx = low.indexOf(t, i)) !== -1) {
 431:       if (idx > i) node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(plain.slice(i, idx)));
 432:       const mark = document.createElement("mark");
 433:       mark.textContent = plain.slice(idx, idx + term.length);
 434:       node.appendChild(mark);
 435:       i = idx + term.length;
 436:     }
 437:     if (i < plain.length) node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(plain.slice(i)));
 438:   }
 439: 
 440:   function applySearchToRow(row) {
 441:     const term = state.searchTerm;
 442:     const entry = state.utterances.get(Number(row.dataset.seq));
 443:     if (!entry) return;
 444:     const rec = entry.record;
 445:     const haystack = [rec.text, rec.translation, rec.speaker].filter(Boolean).join(" ").toLowerCase();
 446:     const match = !term || haystack.includes(term.toLowerCase());
 447:     row.classList.toggle("hidden", !match);
 448:     if (match && term) {
 449:       highlight(row.querySelector(".text"), rec.text || "", term);
 450:       const tr = row.querySelector(".tr");
 451:       if (rec.translation) highlight(tr, rec.translation, term);
 452:     } else {
 453:       // Reset highlight when no term.
 454:       const text = row.querySelector(".text");
 455:       if (text.querySelector("mark")) text.textContent = rec.text || "";
 456:       const tr = row.querySelector(".tr");
 457:       if (tr.querySelector("mark") && rec.translation) tr.textContent = rec.translation;
 458:     }
 459:   }
 460: 
 461:   function applySearchAll() {
 462:     for (const { el: row } of state.utterances.values()) applySearchToRow(row);
 463:   }
 464: 
 465:   el.xSearch.addEventListener("input", () => {
 466:     state.searchTerm = el.xSearch.value.trim();
 467:     applySearchAll();
 468:   });
 469: 
 470:   /* ============================ RECORDING CONTROLS ============================ */
 471:   async function toggleRecording() {
 472:     if (state.recording) {
 473:       try {
 474:         const r = await api("/api/capture/stop", { method: "POST" });
 475:         setRecording(false);
 476:         renderStatus({ recording: false });
 477:         notice("Recording stopped." + (r && r.finalized ? " Session finalized." : ""), "info");
 478:         loadSessionsInto();  // refresh sessions list if drawer open
 479:       } catch (e) {
 480:         notice("Couldn't stop recording: " + (e.message || e), "error");
 481:       }
 482:     } else {
 483:       if (!state.consentOk) { openConsent(); return; }
 484:       try {
 485:         const title = el.xTitle.value.trim() || undefined;
 486:         const r = await api("/api/capture/start", { method: "POST", body: { title } });
 487:         state.sessionId = r && r.session_id;
 488:         setRecording(true);
 489:         renderStatus({ recording: true });
 490:         notice("Recording started.", "info");
 491:       } catch (e) {
 492:         if (e.status === 403) { openConsent(); notice("Please acknowledge consent before recording.", "warn"); }
 493:         else notice("Couldn't start recording: " + (e.message || e), "error");
 494:       }
 495:     }
 496:   }
 497:   el.cToggle.addEventListener("click", toggleRecording);
 498:   el.xToggle.addEventListener("click", toggleRecording);
 499: 
 500:   // Session title -> PUT settings? Title belongs to the session; persist on blur if recording.
 501:   el.xTitle.addEventListener("change", () => {
 502:     // Title is captured at start; here we just keep it local. Server owns session title.
 503:   });
 504: 
 505:   /* ============================ EXPAND / COLLAPSE ============================ */
 506:   el.cExpand.addEventListener("click", () => setView("expanded"));
 507:   el.xCollapse.addEventListener("click", () => setView("compact"));
 508: 
 509:   /* ============================ CONSENT ============================ */
 510:   function openConsent() { el.consent.hidden = false; }
 511:   el.consentAgree.addEventListener("click", async () => {
 512:     try {
 513:       await api("/api/settings", { method: "PUT", body: { consent_acknowledged: true } });
 514:       state.consentOk = true;
 515:       refreshToggleEnabled();
 516:       el.consent.hidden = true;
 517:     } catch (e) {
 518:       notice("Couldn't save consent: " + (e.message || e), "error");
 519:     }
 520:   });
 521: 
 522:   /* ============================ PREFLIGHT ============================ */
 523:   // Interpret a preflight field into a badge class + label + detail.
 524:   function pfBadge(ok) {
 525:     if (ok === true) return "ok";
 526:     if (ok === false) return "fail";
 527:     return "unknown";
 528:   }
 529:   function pfSymbol(cls) {
 530:     return cls === "ok" ? "âœ“" : cls === "fail" ? "!" : cls === "warn" ? "!" : "?";
 531:   }
 532:   function pfRow(label, cls, detail) {
 533:     const row = document.createElement("div");
 534:     row.className = "pf-row";
 535:     const badge = document.createElement("span");
 536:     badge.className = "pf-badge " + cls;
 537:     badge.textContent = pfSymbol(cls);
 538:     const lbl = document.createElement("span");
 539:     lbl.className = "pf-label";
 540:     lbl.textContent = label;
 541:     const det = document.createElement("span");
 542:     det.className = "pf-detail";
 543:     det.textContent = detail || "";
 544:     row.append(badge, lbl, det);
 545:     return row;
 546:   }
 547: 
 548:   async function runPreflight() {
 549:     el.pfRows.textContent = "Checkingâ€¦";
 550:     let pf;
 551:     try { pf = await api("/api/preflight"); }
 552:     catch (e) { el.pfRows.textContent = ""; el.pfRows.appendChild(pfRow("Preflight request failed", "fail", e.message || "")); return; }
 553: 
 554:     el.pfRows.textContent = "";
 555:     // CUDA (+ version)
 556:     el.pfRows.appendChild(pfRow(
 557:       "CUDA GPU acceleration", pfBadge(pf.cuda),
 558:       pf.cuda ? (pf.cuda_version ? `CUDA ${pf.cuda_version}` : "available")
 559:               : (pf.cuda === false ? "not detected â€” CPU fallback" : "unknown")));
 560:     // Whisper loadable
 561:     el.pfRows.appendChild(pfRow("Whisper model loadable", pfBadge(pf.whisper_loadable),
 562:       pf.whisper_loadable === false ? "model failed to load" : ""));
 563:     // Model cache + disk
 564:     {
 565:       const cls = pf.model_cache ? "ok" : "warn";
 566:       const disk = pf.disk_free_gb != null ? `${pf.disk_free_gb} GB free` : "";
 567:       const cacheTxt = pf.model_cache
 568:         ? (typeof pf.model_cache === "string" ? pf.model_cache : "cached")
 569:         : "model not yet cached (will download on first use)";
 570:       el.pfRows.appendChild(pfRow("Model cache & disk space", cls, [cacheTxt, disk].filter(Boolean).join(" Â· ")));
 571:     }
 572:     // HF terms
 573:     el.pfRows.appendChild(pfRow("Hugging Face model terms", pf.hf_terms_ok ? "ok" : "warn",
 574:       pf.hf_terms_ok ? "accepted" : "not accepted â€” some models may be unavailable"));
 575:     // CLI availability
 576:     el.pfRows.appendChild(pfRow("Command-line tools", pfBadge(pf.cli_available),
 577:       pf.cli_available === false ? "not on PATH" : ""));
 578: 
 579:     // Preset explanation
 580:     el.pfPreset.className = "pf-preset";
 581:     el.pfPreset.textContent = "";
 582:     const preset = pf.preset || (state.settings && state.settings.hardware_preset) || "unknown";
 583:     const name = document.createElement("div");
 584:     name.innerHTML = `Auto-selected hardware preset: <span class="p-name"></span>`;
 585:     name.querySelector(".p-name").textContent = preset;
 586:     el.pfPreset.appendChild(name);
 587:     const explain = document.createElement("div");
 588:     explain.className = "muted";
 589:     explain.style.marginTop = "4px";
 590:     explain.textContent = presetExplain(preset);
 591:     el.pfPreset.appendChild(explain);
 592:     if (String(preset).toLowerCase() === "cpu") {
 593:       el.pfPreset.classList.add("cpu-warn");
 594:       const w = document.createElement("div");
 595:       w.className = "warn-text";
 596:       w.textContent = "No GPU detected. Real-time transcription, live translation, and speaker labels are limited on CPU; transcripts may lag or run after the session.";
 597:       el.pfPreset.appendChild(w);
 598:     }
 599:   }
 600:   function presetExplain(p) {
 601:     switch (String(p).toLowerCase()) {
 602:       case "cpu": return "Chosen because no supported GPU was found.";
 603:       case "cuda": case "gpu": return "A CUDA GPU was found and will be used for real-time transcription.";
 604:       default: return "Selected automatically based on the detected hardware.";
 605:     }
 606:   }
 607:   el.pfRefresh.addEventListener("click", runPreflight);
 608:   el.pfContinue.addEventListener("click", () => { el.preflight.hidden = true; setView("compact"); });
 609: 
 610:   /* ============================ SETTINGS DRAWER ============================ */
 611:   function openSettings() {
 612:     el.settings.hidden = false;
 613:     buildSettings();
 614:   }
 615:   el.xSettings.addEventListener("click", openSettings);
 616:   el.setClose.addEventListener("click", () => { el.settings.hidden = true; });
 617:   el.settings.addEventListener("click", (e) => { if (e.target === el.settings) el.settings.hidden = true; });
 618: 
 619:   // Small builders for settings controls.
 620:   function rowSelect(label, sub, value, options, onChange) {
 621:     const row = mkRow(label, sub);
 622:     const sel = document.createElement("select");
 623:     for (const opt of options) {
 624:       const o = document.createElement("option");
 625:       o.value = typeof opt === "string" ? opt : opt.value;
 626:       o.textContent = typeof opt === "string" ? opt : opt.label;
 627:       if (o.value === String(value)) o.selected = true;
 628:       sel.appendChild(o);
 629:     }
 630:     sel.addEventListener("change", () => onChange(sel.value));
 631:     row.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(sel);
 632:     return row;
 633:   }
 634:   function rowToggle(label, sub, checked, onChange) {
 635:     const row = mkRow(label, sub);
 636:     const wrap = document.createElement("label");
 637:     wrap.className = "switch";
 638:     const input = document.createElement("input");
 639:     input.type = "checkbox";
 640:     input.checked = !!checked;
 641:     const track = document.createElement("span");
 642:     track.className = "track";
 643:     wrap.append(input, track);
 644:     input.addEventListener("change", () => onChange(input.checked));
 645:     row.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(wrap);
 646:     return row;
 647:   }
 648:   function rowText(label, sub, value, onCommit, opts = {}) {
 649:     const row = mkRow(label, sub);
 650:     const input = document.createElement("input");
 651:     input.type = opts.number ? "number" : "text";
 652:     input.value = value != null ? value : "";
 653:     if (opts.min != null) input.min = opts.min;
 654:     input.addEventListener("change", () => onCommit(opts.number ? Number(input.value) : input.value));
 655:     row.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(input);
 656:     return row;
 657:   }
 658:   function rowReadonly(label, sub, value) {
 659:     const row = mkRow(label, sub);
 660:     row.classList.add("readonly");
 661:     const v = document.createElement("span");
 662:     v.className = "val";
 663:     v.textContent = value != null && value !== "" ? String(value) : "â€”";
 664:     row.querySelector(".ctl").appendChild(v);
 665:     return row;
 666:   }
 667:   function mkRow(label, sub) {
 668:     const row = document.createElement("div");
 669:     row.className = "set-row";
 670:     const l = document.createElement("div");
 671:     l.className = "lbl";
 672:     l.textContent = label;
 673:     if (sub) { const s = document.createElement("small"); s.textContent = sub; l.appendChild(s); }
 674:     const c = document.createElement("div");
 675:     c.className = "ctl";
 676:     row.append(l, c);
 677:     return row;
 678:   }
 679:   function group(title) {
 680:     const g = document.createElement("div");
 681:     g.className = "set-group";
 682:     const h = document.createElement("h3");
 683:     h.textContent = title;
 684:     g.appendChild(h);
 685:     return g;
 686:   }
 687: 
 688:   async function putSetting(patch) {
 689:     try {
 690:       const updated = await api("/api/settings", { method: "PUT", body: patch });
 691:       state.settings = updated || Object.assign(state.settings || {}, patch);
 692:       applyTheme();
 693:     } catch (e) {
 694:       notice("Couldn't save setting: " + (e.message || e), "error");
 695:     }
 696:   }
 697: 
 698:   function buildSettings() {
 699:     const s = state.settings || {};
 700:     el.setBody.textContent = "";
 701: 
 702:     /* --- Hardware --- */
 703:     const gHw = group("Hardware");
 704:     gHw.appendChild(rowReadonly("Detected preset", "auto-selected at launch", s.hardware_preset));
 705:     gHw.appendChild(rowSelect("Whisper model", null, s.whisper_model,
 706:       ["tiny", "base", "small", "medium", "large-v3", s.whisper_model].filter(uniq),
 707:       (v) => putSetting({ whisper_model: v })));
 708:     gHw.appendChild(rowSelect("Compute type", null, s.whisper_compute_type,
 709:       ["int8", "int8_float16", "float16", "float32", s.whisper_compute_type].filter(uniq),
 710:       (v) => putSetting({ whisper_compute_type: v })));
 711:     gHw.appendChild(rowSelect("Latency mode", null, s.latency_mode,
 712:       ["low", "balanced", "accurate", s.latency_mode].filter(uniq),
 713:       (v) => putSetting({ latency_mode: v })));
 714:     el.setBody.appendChild(gHw);
 715: 
 716:     /* --- Translation --- */
 717:     const gTr = group("Translation");
 718:     gTr.appendChild(rowToggle("Translate", "show a translation line under each utterance",
 719:       s.translate_enabled, (v) => putSetting({ translate_enabled: v })));
 720:     gTr.appendChild(rowSelect("Provider", null, s.translation_provider,
 721:       ["local", "gemini", s.translation_provider].filter(uniq),
 722:       (v) => putSetting({ translation_provider: v })));
 723:     gTr.appendChild(rowSelect("Device", null, s.translation_device,
 724:       ["auto", "cuda", "cpu", s.translation_device].filter(uniq),
 725:       (v) => putSetting({ translation_device: v })));
 726:     el.setBody.appendChild(gTr);
 727: 
 728:     /* --- Speakers --- */
 729:     const gSp = group("Speaker labels");
 730:     gSp.appendChild(rowToggle("Diarization", "identify who is speaking",
 731:       s.diarization_enabled, (v) => putSetting({ diarization_enabled: v })));
 732:     gSp.appendChild(rowToggle("Real-time labels", "label speakers live (needs GPU)",
 733:       s.diarization_realtime, (v) => putSetting({ diarization_realtime: v })));
 734:     el.setBody.appendChild(gSp);
 735: 
 736:     /* --- Storage --- */
 737:     const gStore = group("Storage");
 738:     gStore.appendChild(rowText("Sessions folder", null, s.sessions_root,
 739:       (v) => putSetting({ sessions_root: v })));

 793:   function uniq(v, i, arr) { return v != null && v !== "" && arr.indexOf(v) === i; }
 794: 
 795:   function secretRow(label, name, isSet) {
 796:     const row = mkRow(label, isSet ? null : "not configured");
 797:     const state_span = document.createElement("span");
 798:     state_span.className = "secret-state " + (isSet ? "set" : "unset");
 799:     state_span.textContent = isSet ? "is set" : "not set";
 800:     const setBtn = document.createElement("button");
 801:     setBtn.className = "link-btn";
 802:     setBtn.textContent = isSet ? "Replace" : "Set";
 803:     setBtn.addEventListener("click", () => setSecret(name, label));
 804:     const ctl = row.querySelector(".ctl");
 805:     ctl.appendChild(state_span);
 806:     ctl.appendChild(setBtn);
 807:     if (isSet) {
 808:       const clrBtn = document.createElement("button");
 809:       clrBtn.className = "link-btn danger-link";
 810:       clrBtn.style.color = "var(--fail)";
 811:       clrBtn.textContent = "Clear";
 812:       clrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => clearSecret(name, label));
 813:       ctl.appendChild(clrBtn);
 814:     }
 815:     return row;
 816:   }
 817: 
 818:   async function setSecret(name, label) {
 819:     const value = window.prompt(`Enter ${label}. It is stored write-only and never shown again.`);
 820:     if (value == null) return;
 821:     const v = value.trim();
 822:     if (!v) return;
 823:     try {
 824:       await api(`/api/secrets/${name}`, { method: "POST", body: { value: v } });
 825:       notice(`${label} saved.`, "info");
 826:       await refreshSettings();
 827:       buildSettings();
 828:     } catch (e) { notice(`Couldn't save ${label}: ` + (e.message || e), "error"); }
 829:   }
 830:   async function clearSecret(name, label) {
 831:     confirmDialog(`Clear the stored ${label}? This cannot be undone.`, "Clear", async () => {
 832:       try {
 833:         await api(`/api/secrets/${name}`, { method: "DELETE" });
 834:         notice(`${label} cleared.`, "info");
 835:         await refreshSettings();
 836:         buildSettings();
 837:       } catch (e) { notice(`Couldn't clear ${label}: ` + (e.message || e), "error"); }
 838:     });
 839:   }
 840: 
 841:   /* ============================ SESSIONS LIST ============================ */
 842:   async function loadSessionsInto() {
 843:     const list = document.getElementById("sess-list");
 844:     if (!list) return; // drawer not open
 845:     let sessions;
 846:     try { sessions = await api("/api/sessions"); }
 847:     catch (e) { list.textContent = ""; const p = document.createElement("div"); p.className = "sess-empty"; p.textContent = "Couldn't load sessions."; list.appendChild(p); return; }
 848: 
 849:     list.textContent = "";
 850:     if (!sessions || !sessions.length) {
 851:       const p = document.createElement("div");
 852:       p.className = "sess-empty";
 853:       p.textContent = "No sessions yet.";
 854:       list.appendChild(p);
 855:       return;
 856:     }
 857:     for (const meta of sessions) {
 858:       list.appendChild(sessionItem(meta));
 859:     }
 860:   }
 861: 
 862:   function sessionItem(meta) {
 863:     const item = document.createElement("div");
 864:     item.className = "sess-item";
 865:     const top = document.createElement("div");
 866:     top.className = "s-top";
 867:     const title = document.createElement("div");
 868:     title.className = "s-title";
 869:     title.textContent = meta.title || "Untitled session";
 870:     const m = document.createElement("div");
 871:     m.className = "s-meta";
 872:     m.textContent = [fmtDate(meta.created_at), fmtDur(meta.duration_sec)].filter(Boolean).join(" Â· ");
 873:     top.append(title, m);
 874: 
 875:     const actions = document.createElement("div");
 876:     actions.className = "s-actions";
 877:     const delAudio = document.createElement("button");
 878:     delAudio.className = "link-btn";
 879:     delAudio.textContent = "Delete audio only";
 880:     delAudio.addEventListener("click", () => {
 881:       confirmDialog(`Delete the audio file for "${meta.title || "this session"}"? The transcript is kept.`, "Delete audio", async () => {
 882:         try { await api(`/api/sessions/${meta.session_id}/audio`, { method: "DELETE" }); notice("Audio deleted.", "info"); }
 883:         catch (e) { notice("Couldn't delete audio: " + (e.message || e), "error"); }
 884:       });
 885:     });
 886:     const delAll = document.createElement("button");
 887:     delAll.className = "link-btn danger-link";
 888:     delAll.style.color = "var(--fail)";
 889:     delAll.textContent = "Delete session";
 890:     delAll.addEventListener("click", () => {
 891:       confirmDialog(`Permanently delete "${meta.title || "this session"}" and its transcript? This cannot be undone.`, "Delete", async () => {
 892:         try { await api(`/api/sessions/${meta.session_id}`, { method: "DELETE" }); notice("Session deleted.", "info"); loadSessionsInto(); }
 893:         catch (e) { notice("Couldn't delete session: " + (e.message || e), "error"); }
 894:       });
 895:     });
 896:     actions.append(delAudio, delAll);
 897:     item.append(top, actions);
 898:     return item;
 899:   }
 900: 
 901:   /* ============================ CONFIRM DIALOG ============================ */
 902:   let confirmHandler = null;
 903:   function confirmDialog(message, okLabel, onOk) {
 904:     el.confirmMsg.textContent = message;
 905:     el.confirmOk.textContent = okLabel || "Confirm";
 906:     confirmHandler = onOk;
 907:     el.confirm.hidden = false;
 908:   }
 909:   el.confirmCancel.addEventListener("click", () => { el.confirm.hidden = true; confirmHandler = null; });
 910:   el.confirmOk.addEventListener("click", async () => {
 911:     const h = confirmHandler;
 912:     el.confirm.hidden = true; confirmHandler = null;
 913:     if (h) await h();
 914:   });
 915: 
 916:   /* ============================ THEME ============================ */
 917:   function applyTheme() {
 918:     const theme = (state.settings && state.settings.theme) || "auto";
 919:     document.documentElement.setAttribute("data-theme", theme);
 920:   }
 921: 
 922:   /* ============================ WEBSOCKET ============================ */
 923:   function wsUrl() {
 924:     const proto = window.location.protocol === "https:" ? "wss:" : "ws:";
 925:     return `${proto}//${window.location.host}/ws?token=${encodeURIComponent(TOKEN || "")}`;
 926:   }
 927: 
 928:   function connectWs() {
 929:     let ws;
 930:     try { ws = new WebSocket(wsUrl()); }
 931:     catch (e) { scheduleReconnect(); return; }
 932:     state.ws = ws;
 933: 
 934:     ws.addEventListener("open", () => {
 935:       state.wsBackoff = 500;
 936:       // On reconnect, replay durable events we missed by seq.
 937:       if (state.everConnected && state.lastSeq > 0) catchUp(state.lastSeq);
 938:       state.everConnected = true;
 939:       // Ask the server for a fresh status snapshot.
 940:       requestStatus();
 941:     });
 942: 
 943:     ws.addEventListener("message", (ev) => {
 944:       let msg;
 945:       try { msg = JSON.parse(ev.data); } catch (_) { return; }
 946:       handleWsMessage(msg);
 947:     });
 948: 
 949:     ws.addEventListener("close", () => { state.ws = null; scheduleReconnect(); });
 950:     ws.addEventListener("error", () => { try { ws.close(); } catch (_) {} });
 951:   }
 952: 
 953:   function scheduleReconnect() {
 954:     clearTimeout(state.wsTimer);
 955:     const delay = state.wsBackoff;
 956:     state.wsBackoff = Math.min(state.wsBackoff * 2, 10000);
 957:     state.wsTimer = setTimeout(connectWs, delay);
 958:   }
 959: 
 960:   function requestStatus() {
 961:     // Prefer a WS request; fall back to REST if the socket isn't open.
 962:     if (state.ws && state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
 963:       try { state.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "get_status" })); return; } catch (_) {}
 964:     }
 965:     api("/api/capture/status").then(applyStatusSnapshot).catch(() => {});
 966:   }
 967: 
 968:   function applyStatusSnapshot(st) {
 969:     if (!st) return;
 970:     if (typeof st.recording === "boolean") setRecording(st.recording);
 971:     if (st.session_id) state.sessionId = st.session_id;
 972:     renderStatus(st);
 973:   }
 974: 
 975:   function handleWsMessage(msg) {
 976:     switch (msg.type) {
 977:       case "utterance":
 978:         if (msg.record) addUtterance(msg.record);
 979:         break;
 980:       case "patch":
 981:         if (msg.seq != null) {
 982:           if (msg.seq > state.lastSeq) state.lastSeq = msg.seq;
 983:           patchUtterance(msg.seq, msg.fields || {});
 984:         }
 985:         break;
 986:       case "status":
 987:         if (typeof msg.recording === "boolean") setRecording(msg.recording);
 988:         renderStatus(msg);
 989:         break;
 990:       case "rename":
 991:         renameSpeaker(msg.old, msg.new);
 992:         break;
 993:       case "error":
 994:         notice(`${msg.message || "Error"}${msg.code ? " (" + msg.code + ")" : ""}`, "error");
 995:         break;
 996:       default:
 997:         // Unknown message types are ignored on purpose.
 998:         break;
 999:     }
1000:   }
1001: 
1002:   // Replay durable utterance events by seq after a reconnect gap.
1003:   async function catchUp(sinceSeq) {
1004:     if (!state.sessionId) return;
1005:     try {
1006:       const rows = await api(`/api/sessions/${state.sessionId}/utterances?since_seq=${sinceSeq}`);
1007:       if (Array.isArray(rows)) for (const rec of rows) addUtterance(rec);
1008:     } catch (e) { /* best-effort catch-up */ }
1009:   }
1010: 
1011:   /* ============================ BOOT ============================ */
1012:   async function refreshSettings() {
1013:     state.settings = await api("/api/settings");
1014:     state.consentOk = !!(state.settings && state.settings.consent_acknowledged);
1015:     applyTheme();
1016:     refreshToggleEnabled();
1017:     return state.settings;
1018:   }
1019: 
1020:   async function boot() {
1021:     if (!TOKEN) { el.noToken.hidden = false; return; }
1022:     el.app.hidden = false;
1023: 
1024:     // 1) Settings drive consent + theme.
1025:     try {
1026:       await refreshSettings();
1027:     } catch (e) {
1028:       notice("Couldn't reach the local service. Some features may be unavailable.", "error");
1029:       state.settings = {};
1030:     }
1031: 
1032:     // 2) Consent gate.
1033:     if (!state.consentOk) openConsent();
1034: 
1035:     // 3) Preflight before the first record.
1036:     el.preflight.hidden = false;
1037:     runPreflight();
1038: 
1039:     // 4) Current capture status (in case a session is already running).
1040:     try { applyStatusSnapshot(await api("/api/capture/status")); } catch (_) {}
1041:     renderRecent();
1042: 
1043:     // 5) Live stream.
1044:     connectWs();
1045: 
1046:     // Default to compact bar underneath the overlays.
1047:     setView("compact");
1048:   }
1049: 
1050:   document.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
1051:     if (e.key === "Escape") {
1052:       if (!el.settings.hidden) el.settings.hidden = true;
1053:       else if (!el.confirm.hidden) { el.confirm.hidden = true; confirmHandler = null; }
1054:     }
1055:   });
1056: 
1057:   boot();
1058: })();

> docs\SPEC.md:6:**Status:** Design resolved. v2 integrates the `codex-spec-review-01` adversarial review: hardware 
presets replace fixed defaults, the timebase is sample-accurate, crash-safety and local-API protection are hardened, 
the summarizer is sandboxed, and the build is sequenced into gated milestones (M1–M4). An engineer can build v1 
directly from this document via the M1→M4 milestone plan (§12). Genuinely hardware-dependent unknowns (real loopback 
reliability across drivers, real-world RTF on the 4070) are deferred to the benchmark harness (§9), not left as open 
design questions.
  docs\SPEC.md:7:
> docs\SPEC.md:27:Everything runs **on the user's machine**. No audio or transcript leaves the computer except when 
the user explicitly invokes a cloud-based translation or summarization provider (both off/local by default).
  docs\SPEC.md:28:
> docs\SPEC.md:49:- On-demand post-meeting AI summarization via pluggable CLI/local providers (Claude CLI default), 
**hardened against prompt injection** (§5.6).
  docs\SPEC.md:50:- Durable session storage in `%LOCALAPPDATA%` with crash-safe autosave, a defined JSONL schema 
(`schema: 2`), per-session concurrency locking, and first-class delete + retention (§5.7).
> docs\SPEC.md:164:     summarizer.py  ── reads transcript TEXT (untrusted, sandboxed) ──► summary.md
  docs\SPEC.md:165:     diarizer.py T2 ── reads audio_them.wav (pyannote, SAMPLE time) ──► relabel transcript
> docs\SPEC.md:170:v1 replaced "default `large-v3` fp16 everywhere" with **hardware presets**. Setting 
`hardware_preset` (§7) selects the whole real-time stack. Default is `auto`, which detects VRAM at startup via:
  docs\SPEC.md:171:
> docs\SPEC.md:196:- A user may override individual knobs (`whisper_model`, `whisper_compute_type`, 
`translation_device`, `diarization_device`, `diarization_embedder`) after a preset is chosen; explicit overrides win 
over the preset but are validated against available VRAM with a warning.
  docs\SPEC.md:197:
> docs\SPEC.md:222:- The `patch` message carries the utterance `id`/`seq` and only the changed fields (`translation`, 
`translation_provider`, `translation_error`, `speaker`, `diarization_confidence`, `diarization_source`).
  docs\SPEC.md:223:
> docs\SPEC.md:410:**Responsibility.** Translate foreign utterance text into Vietnamese as a **post-STT patch**, 
within a latency budget, skipping stale work. Pluggable providers.
  docs\SPEC.md:411:
> docs\SPEC.md:428:**When to translate.** Only if ALL: `translate_enabled` true; detected `lang != target_lang` 
(default `vi`); `lang ∈ source_languages` (empty = any non-target). Runs on the `post_worker` **after** the STT text 
is already shown, then emits a `patch`.
  docs\SPEC.md:429:
> docs\SPEC.md:439:- **Quality note (code comment + docs):** NLLB JA→VI is *moderate*; for higher quality the user can 
switch `translation_provider` to `gemini` (sends text to Google — a deliberate quality/privacy tradeoff in Settings).
  docs\SPEC.md:440:
> docs\SPEC.md:471:  - **Embedder from preset (§4.3).** Default on `gpu_12gb` is **Resemblyzer on CPU** (light, 
non-blocking to STT). ECAPA-TDNN (`speechbrain/spkrec-ecapa-voxceleb`) on GPU only if the user opts in 
(`diarization_embedder: "ecapa"` + `diarization_device: "cuda"`), permitted by default only on `gpu_16gb_plus`.
  docs\SPEC.md:472:  - **Model-specific thresholds (replaces single 0.70).** `sim_threshold_ecapa` (default 0.75) and 
`sim_threshold_resemblyzer` (default 0.70); the active threshold is chosen by the embedder in use.
> docs\SPEC.md:473:  - **Confidence.** `confidence = clamp((max_sim − second_best_sim) / margin_scale, 0, 1)` (a 
margin score), or a calibrated distance for a single centroid. Persisted as `diarization_confidence`.
  docs\SPEC.md:474:  - **Algorithm.** Maintain centroids `{label: (mean_embedding, accumulated_speech_sec, count)}`.
> docs\SPEC.md:482:- **Toggle / cost.** `diarization_enabled` (default ON) + `diarization_realtime` (default ON, but 
the ladder or `cpu` preset can turn it off). When off, "Them" utterances are labelled `"Them"`. The ladder (§4.4) 
moves the embedder to CPU then disables it before touching STT.
  docs\SPEC.md:483:
> docs\SPEC.md:488:- **Relabelling in sample time (§4.8).** pyannote returns speaker-homogeneous spans on the 
`audio_them.wav` timeline. For each "them" utterance, compute overlap-weighted majority pyannote speaker over 
`[audio_start_sample, audio_end_sample]` **converted to that same sample timeline**, and assign a stable label 
(`"Speaker A/B/…"`). **Where pyannote reports overlapping speakers** over the span, preserve multiple candidates 
(store primary + `speaker_alt`) and mark `is_overlap`. Set `diarization_source="offline"`. Rewrite `transcript.jsonl` 
atomically (backup `transcript.jsonl.pre-rediarize`), re-render `transcript.md`.
  docs\SPEC.md:489:- Long-running: background thread; progress/completion via WS `rediarize` + REST status.
> docs\SPEC.md:491:### 5.6 `summarizer.py` — AI summarization (hardened, untrusted input)
  docs\SPEC.md:492:
> docs\SPEC.md:497:class Summarizer(Protocol):
  docs\SPEC.md:498:    def summarize(self, transcript_text: str, meta: dict) -> str: ...  # returns markdown
> docs\SPEC.md:501:class ClaudeCliSummarizer(Summarizer):   # default
> docs\SPEC.md:502:class CodexCliSummarizer(Summarizer):
> docs\SPEC.md:503:class GeminiSummarizer(Summarizer):
> docs\SPEC.md:504:class OllamaSummarizer(Summarizer):
  docs\SPEC.md:505:```
> docs\SPEC.md:507:**Selection.** `summarizer_provider`: `"claude_cli" | "codex_cli" | "gemini" | "ollama"`, default 
`"claude_cli"` (per user request).
  docs\SPEC.md:508:
> docs\SPEC.md:509:**Input assembly.** Plain-text transcript from `transcript.jsonl`, one line per utterance: 
`"[mm:ss] <SpeakerLabel>: <text>"`, using Vietnamese translation where present (`summary_use_translation`, default 
true) else original. Short header (title/date/duration/languages). Adjacent `forced_cut` chunks from the same speaker 
may be stitched into one line. If very long, chunk to `summary_max_chars` (default 48000) map-reduce.
  docs\SPEC.md:510:
> docs\SPEC.md:518:- `subprocess.run([...], input=payload, capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", 
timeout=summary_timeout_s, cwd=<isolated temp dir>, creationflags=subprocess.CREATE_NO_WINDOW, env=<minimal env>)`. 
Isolated cwd so any accidental file write lands in a throwaway dir.
> docs\SPEC.md:519:- **Residual risk (documented):** even sandboxed, an agentic CLI processing attacker-controlled 
text carries residual prompt-injection risk (at worst a misleading summary; with tools truly disabled, no side 
effects). The **safest** option for untrusted transcripts is a **non-agentic** provider — **Gemini** (API, no local 
tools) or **Ollama** (local, no tools). These are one setting away. Claude CLI remains the **default per user 
preference**, run in the hardened no-tools mode above; the Settings help text states this tradeoff plainly.
  docs\SPEC.md:520:
> docs\SPEC.md:521:**Provider implementations.**
> docs\SPEC.md:522:- **ClaudeCliSummarizer (default):** as above (stdin, no-tools print mode, isolated cwd, 
`CREATE_NO_WINDOW`).
> docs\SPEC.md:523:- **CodexCliSummarizer:** `codex exec` read-only sandbox, prompt+transcript via stdin.
> docs\SPEC.md:524:- **GeminiSummarizer:** Gemini API (needs `gemini_api_key`); no local tools — recommended for 
untrusted transcripts.
> docs\SPEC.md:525:- **OllamaSummarizer:** POST `http://localhost:11434/api/generate`, model `ollama_model` (default 
`llama3.1`); local, no tools.
  docs\SPEC.md:526:
> docs\SPEC.md:527:**Availability & output.** `available()` probes CLI presence (`shutil.which`) / key / Ollama 
reachability; if unavailable, `summarize()` isn't called and the UI shows a clear notice naming an alternative 
provider. On success, save to `<session_dir>/summary.md` (keep `.bak`), return over REST/WS, record `summary_provider` 
+ `summarized_at` in meta.
  docs\SPEC.md:528:
> docs\SPEC.md:611:  "translation_provider": "nllb",
  docs\SPEC.md:612:  "translation_error": false,
> docs\SPEC.md:616:  "diarization_source": "realtime",
> docs\SPEC.md:617:  "diarization_confidence": 0.82,

> docs\SPEC.md:627:- **New in schema 2** (vs schema 1): `speaker_alt`, `audio_start_sample`, `audio_end_sample`, 
`source_epoch_id`, `source_offset_sec`, `forced_cut`, `translation_error`(bool now explicit), `stale_skipped`, 
`diarization_confidence`, `is_overlap`, `forced_overflow`, `effective_model`, `effective_compute_type`, 
`stt_latency_ms`. (`diarization_source` and `translation_provider` already existed.)
> docs\SPEC.md:628:- **Migration from schema 1.** A reader that encounters `"schema": 1` records fills the new fields 
with safe defaults: sample fields `null`, `source_epoch_id: 0`, `source_offset_sec: 0.0`, 
`forced_cut/is_overlap/forced_overflow/stale_skipped: false`, `diarization_confidence: null`, 
`effective_model/compute_type` from `meta.json`, `stt_latency_ms: null`, `speaker_alt: null`. Records are up-converted 
lazily on next rewrite. `load_session` tolerates mixed-schema files.

> docs\SPEC.md:727:**Preflight / readiness screen** (first run, and before the first record of a session). Calls `GET 
/api/preflight` and shows pass/warn/fail for: CUDA available + version; faster-whisper model loads; model cache 
present + estimated download size + **free disk space**; HF terms/token for tier-2; summarizer CLI availability; and 
the **auto-selected `hardware_preset`** with an explanation. The user proceeds, or fixes issues (install a CLI, free 
disk, add a token). On the `cpu` preset it warns clearly that real-time features are limited.

> docs\SPEC.md:742:  - `hardware_preset` (auto / cpu / gpu_8gb / gpu_12gb / gpu_16gb_plus) with the detected VRAM

> docs\SPEC.md:744:  - Translate on/off; source-language chips; translation provider; translation device.

  docs\SPEC.md:746:  - Diarization on/off; realtime on/off; embedder; device; thresholds.
